item.title: VyOS: Configurar un Servidor DHCP con Ejemplos Prácticos
item.description: Guía práctica para configurar el servidor DHCP en VyOS: subredes, opciones avanzadas, reservas estáticas y troubleshooting.

item.title: VyOS: Primera Configuración — NAT, Firewall, Rutas Estáticas y VLANs
item.description: Guía práctica para dejar un router VyOS funcional desde cero: NAT masquerade, zonas de firewall, rutas estáticas y subinterfaces 802.1Q.

item.title: Dual WAN en MikroTik RouterOS v7: Balanceo y Failover con PCC
item.description: Configuración completa de balanceo de carga y failover sobre dos enlaces WAN en MikroTik RouterOS v7 usando PCC, mangle, routing tables y netwatch.

item.title: OPNsense: 5 VLANs, Aislamiento y Seguridad de Perímetro
item.description: Configuración completa de 5 VLANs en OPNsense: asignación de IPs, aislamiento inter-VLAN vía firewall, permitir internet bloqueando RFC 1918, y hardening del perímetro.

item.title: Subnetting: From First Principles to VLSM
item.description: A practical subnetting guide covering binary math, CIDR notation, VLSM, and common exam-style calculations — written for engineers who want to understand the why.

item.title: Home Network Lab
item.description: My home lab setup — routers, switches, and cable chaos.

item.title: BGP Graceful Restart: What It Is and When It Actually Helps
item.description: A practical look at BGP Graceful Restart — the mechanism, its limitations, and when it genuinely improves convergence vs. when it's just theater.

item.title: Network Automation with Ansible: A Practical Starting Point
item.description: Getting started with Ansible for network automation — without the cargo-culting.

item.title: OSPF LSA Types: A Field Reference
item.description: A concise reference for OSPF LSA types 1–7, what they carry, and which routers originate them.

item.title: About
item.description:
Hey — I’m a network engineer with a focus on large-scale infrastructure, routing protocols, and network automation.

I write about things I’m learning, breaking, and occasionally fixing.

What I work with

  • Routing: BGP, OSPF, IS-IS
  • Automation: Ansible, Python (netmiko, NAPALM, pyATS)
  • Cloud networking: AWS VPC, Azure VNET, Transit Gateway
  • Vendors: Cisco IOS/IOS-XE/NX-OS, Juniper Junos, Arista EOS
  • Monitoring: Prometheus, Grafana, SNMP, gNMI/gRPC

Why this blog

The internet is held together by people who spend their days staring at BGP tables, writing Jinja2 templates, and arguing about whether OSPF area 0 should span the DC fabric. This is a place for those people.
